name: requests-html-playwright

metadata.name: requests-html-playwright
metadata.summary: Requests-HTML(with microsoft/playwright-python): HTML Parsing for Humans™

documentation:
![image](https://github.com/Asugawara/requests-html-playwright/actions/workflows/run_test.yml/badge.svg) ![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/requests-html-playwright?color=green) ![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/requests-html-playwright) ![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/license/Asugawara/requests-html-playwright) # Requests-HTML(with microsoft/playwright-python): HTML Parsing for Humans™ This library intends to make parsing HTML (e.g. scraping the web) as simple and intuitive as possible. When using this library you automatically get: - **Full JavaScript support**! (Using Chromium(Webkit, Firefox), thanks to playwright) - *CSS Selectors* (a.k.a jQuery-style, thanks to PyQuery). - *XPath Selectors*, for the faint of heart. - Mocked user-agent (like a real web browser). - Automatic following of redirects. - Connection--pooling and cookie persistence. - The Requests experience you know and love, with magical parsing abilities. - **Async Support** # Installation ```bash $ pip install requests-html-playwright ✨🍰✨ ``` Only **Python 3.8 and above** is supported. # Tutorial & Usage Make a GET request to \'python.org\', using Requests: ```python >>> from requests_html_playwright import HTMLSession >>> session = HTMLSession() >>> r = session.get('https://python.org/') ``` Try async and get some sites at the same time: ```python >>> from requests_html_playwright import AsyncHTMLSession >>> asession = AsyncHTMLSession() >>> async def get_pythonorg(): ... r = await asession.get('https://python.org/') ... return r ... >>> async def get_reddit(): ... r = await asession.get('https://reddit.com/') ... return r ... >>> async def get_google(): ... r = await asession.get('https://google.com/') ... return r ... >>> results = asession.run(get_pythonorg, get_reddit, get_google) >>> results # check the requests all returned a 200 (success) code [, , ] >>> # Each item in the results list is a response object and can be interacted with as such >>> for result in results: ... print(result.html.url) ... https://www.python.org/ https://www.google.com/ https://www.reddit.com/ ``` Note that the order of the objects in the results list represents the order they were returned in, not the order that the coroutines are passed to the `run` method, which is shown in the example by the order being different. Grab a list of all links on the page, as--is (anchors excluded): ```python >>> r.html.links {'//docs.python.org/3/tutorial/', '/about/apps/', 'https://github.com/python/pythondotorg/issues', 'https://www.python.org/community/sigs/', 'https://www.python.org/events/python-user-group/', 'http://ipython.org', 'https://www.python.org/shell/', 'https://www.python.org/community/irc/', 'http://feedproxy.google.com/~r/PythonInsider/~3/x_c9D0S-4C4/python-370b1-is-now-available-for.html', 'http://wiki.python.org/moin/TkInter', 'https://www.python.org/jobs/', 'https://www.python.org/events/python-events/604/'} ``` Select an element with a CSS Selector: ```python >>> about = r.html.find('#about', first=True) ``` Grab an element\'s text contents: ```python >>> print(about.text) About Applications Quotes Getting Started Help Python Brochure ``` Introspect an Element\'s attributes: ```python >>> about.attrs {'id': 'about', 'class': ('tier-1', 'element-1'), 'aria-haspopup': 'true'} ``` Render out an Element\'s HTML: ```python >>> about.html '
  • \nAbout\n
      \n
    • Applications
      • \n
    • Quotes
      • \n
    • Getting Started
      • \n
    • Help
      • \n
    • Python Brochure
      • \n
    \n
    • ' ``` Select Elements within Elements: ```python >>> about.find('a') [, , , , , ] ``` Search for links within an element: ```python >>> about.absolute_links {'http://brochure.getpython.info/', 'https://www.python.org/about/gettingstarted/', 'https://www.python.org/about/', 'https://www.python.org/about/quotes/', 'https://www.python.org/about/help/', 'https://www.python.org/about/apps/'} ``` Search for text on the page: ```python >>> r.html.search('Python is a {} language')[0] programming ``` More complex CSS Selector example (copied from Chrome dev tools): ```python >>> r = session.get('https://python.org/') >>> sel = '#about > ul > li.tier-2.element-1 > a' >>> print(r.html.find(sel, first=True).text) Applications ``` XPath is also supported: ```python >>> r.html.xpath('//*[@id="about"]/ul/li[1]/a') [] ``` # JavaScript Support Let\'s grab some text that\'s rendered by JavaScript. Until 2020, the Python 2.7 countdown clock () will serve as a good test page: ```python >>> r = session.get('https://pythonclock.org') ``` Let\'s try and see the dynamically rendered code (The countdown clock). To do that quickly at first, we\'ll search between the last text we see before it (\'Python 2.7 will retire in\...\') and the first text we see after it (\'Enable Guido Mode\'). ```python >>> r.html.search('Python 2.7 will retire in...{}Enable Guido Mode')[0] '\n \n
    \n
    \n
    \n
    \n
    1Year2Months28Days16Hours52Minutes46Seconds
    \n
    \n
    \n
    \n' '
    1Year2Months28Days16Hours52Minutes46Seconds
    \n' '
    \n' '
    \n' '

functions.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.user_agent
documentation:
Returns an apparently legit user-agent, if not requested one of a specific
style. Defaults to a Chrome-style User-Agent.

functions.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.response_hook
documentation: Change response encoding and replace it by a HTMLResponse.

classes.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.raw_html
documentation:
Bytes representation of the HTML content.
(`learn more <http://www.diveintopython3.net/strings.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.raw_html
documentation: Property setter for self.html.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.html
documentation:
Unicode representation of the HTML content
(`learn more <http://www.diveintopython3.net/strings.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.encoding
documentation:
The encoding string to be used, extracted from the HTML and
{class}`HTMLResponse <HTMLResponse>` headers.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.encoding
documentation: Property setter for self.encoding.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.pq
documentation:
[PyQuery](https://pythonhosted.org/pyquery/) representation
of the {class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.lxml
documentation:
[lxml](http://lxml.de) representation of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.text
documentation:
The text content of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.full_text
documentation:
The full text content (including links) of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.find
documentation:
Given a CSS Selector, returns a list of
{class}`Element <Element>` objects or a single one.

```
param selector

CSS Selector to use.

param clean

Whether or not to sanitize the found HTML of <script> and <style> tags.

param containing

If specified, only return elements that contain the provided text.

param first

Whether or not to return just the first result.

param _encoding

The encoding format.
```

Example CSS Selectors:

- `a`
- `a.someClass`
- `a#someID`
- `a[target=_blank]`

See W3School's [CSS Selectors Reference](https://www.w3schools.com/cssref/css_selectors.asp)
for more details.

If `first` is `True`, only returns the first
{class}`Element <Element>` found.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.xpath
documentation:
Given an XPath selector, returns a list of
{class}`Element <Element>` objects or a single one.

```
param selector

XPath Selector to use.

param clean

Whether or not to sanitize the found HTML of <script> and <style> tags.

param first

Whether or not to return just the first result.

param _encoding

The encoding format.
```

If a sub-selector is specified (e.g. `//a/@href`), a simple
list of results is returned.

See W3School's [XPath Examples](https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xpath_examples.asp)
for more details.

If `first` is `True`, only returns the first
{class}`Element <Element>` found.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.search
documentation:
Search the {class}`Element <Element>` for the given Parse template.

```
param template

The Parse template to use.
```

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.search_all
documentation:
Search the {class}`Element <Element>` (multiple times) for the given parse
template.

```
param template

The Parse template to use.
```

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.links
documentation: All found links on page, in as–is form.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser._make_absolute
documentation: Makes a given link absolute.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.absolute_links
documentation:
All found links on page, in absolute form
(`learn more <https://www.navegabem.com/absolute-or-relative-links.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.BaseParser.base_url
documentation:
The base URL for the page. Supports the `<base>` tag
([learn more](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/tag_base.asp)).

A basic HTML/Element Parser, for Humans.

```
param element

The element from which to base the parsing upon.

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.

param html

HTML from which to base the parsing upon (optional).

param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.
```

classes.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.Element

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.Element.attrs
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of the attributes of the {class}`Element <Element>`
([learn more](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/ref_attributes.asp)).

An element of HTML.

```
param element

The element from which to base the parsing upon.

param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.
```

classes.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML.next
documentation:
Attempts to find the next page, if there is one. If `fetch`
is `True` (default), returns {class}`HTML <HTML>` object of
next page. If `fetch` is `False`, simply returns the next URL.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML._async_render
documentation: Handle page creation and js rendering. Internal use for arender methods.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML._convert_cookiejar_to_render
documentation: Convert HTMLSession.cookies:cookiejar[] for SetCookieParam

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML._convert_cookiesjar_to_render
documentation:
Convert HTMLSession.cookies for browser.newPage().setCookie
Return a list of dict

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML.render
documentation:
Reloads the response in Chromium, and replaces HTML content
with an updated version, with JavaScript executed.

```
param retries

The number of times to retry loading the page in Chromium.

param script

JavaScript to execute upon page load (optional).

param keep_page

If True will allow you to interact with the browser page through r.html.page.

param send_cookies_session

If True send HTMLSession.cookies convert.
```

If `script` is specified, it will execute the provided JavaScript at
runtime. Example:

```python
script = """
    () => {
        return {
            width: document.documentElement.clientWidth,
            height: document.documentElement.clientHeight,
            deviceScaleFactor: window.devicePixelRatio,
        }
    }
"""
```

Returns the return value of the executed  `script`, if any is provided:

```python
>>> r.html.render(script=script)
{'width': 800, 'height': 600, 'deviceScaleFactor': 1}
```

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTML.arender
documentation: Async version of render. Takes same parameters.

An HTML document, ready for parsing.

```
param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.

param html

HTML from which to base the parsing upon (optional).

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.
```

classes.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTMLResponse
documentation:
An HTML-enabled {class}`requests.Response <requests.Response>` object.
Effectively the same, but with an intelligent `.html` property added.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.HTMLSession.close
documentation: If a browser was created close it first.

classes.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.__init__
documentation:
Set or create an event loop and a thread pool.

```
param loop

Asyncio loop to use.

param workers

Amount of threads to use for executing async calls.
If not pass it will default to the number of processors on the
machine, multiplied by 5.
```

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.request
documentation: Partial original request func and run it in a thread.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.close
documentation: If a browser was created close it first.

methods.name: requests_html_playwright.requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.run
documentation:
Pass in all the coroutines you want to run, it will wrap each one
in a task, run it and wait for the result. Return a list with all
results, this is returned in the same order coros are passed in.
An async consumable session.
